records.ety: [OE. whit, AS. hwīt; akin to OFries. and OS. hwīt, D. wit, G. weiss, OHG. wīz, hwīz, Icel. hvītr, Sw. hvit, Dan. hvid, Goth. hweits, Lith. szveisti, to make bright, Russ. sviet' light, Skr. çvēta white, çvit to be bright. √42. Cf. Wheat, Whitsunday.]

defs: Reflecting to the eye all the rays of the spectrum combined; not tinted with any of the proper colors or their mixtures; having the color of pure snow; snowy; -- the opposite of black or dark; as, white paper; a white skin.
quotes: White as the whitest lily on a stream.

defs: Destitute of color, as in the cheeks, or of the tinge of blood color; pale; pallid; as, white with fear.
quotes: Or whispering with white lips, “The foe! They come! they come!”

defs: Having the color of purity; free from spot or blemish, or from guilt or pollution; innocent; pure.
quotes: White as thy fame, and as thy honor clear.
No whiter page than Addison's remains.

defs: Gray, as from age; having silvery hair; hoary.
quotes: Your high engendered battles 'gainst a head So old and white as this.

defs: Characterized by freedom from that which disturbs, and the like; fortunate; happy; favorable.
quotes: On the whole, however, the dominie reckoned this as one of the white days of his life.

defs: Regarded with especial favor; favorite; darling.
quotes: I am his white boy, and will not be gullet.
Driving their cattle continually with them, and feeding only upon their milk and white meats.
A pistol charged with white powder.
notes: ☞ White is used in many self-explaining compounds, as white-backed, white-bearded, white-footed.

defs: The color of pure snow; one of the natural colors of bodies, yet not strictly a color, but a composition of all colors; the opposite of black; whiteness. See the Note under Color, n., 1.
quotes: Finely attired in a of white.

defs: Something having the color of snow; something white, or nearly so; as, the white of the eye.
Specifically, the central part of the butt in archery, which was formerly painted white; the center of a mark at which a missile is shot.
quotes: 'T was I won the wager, though you hit the white.

defs: A person with a white skin; a member of the white, or Caucasian, races of men.
A white pigment; as, Venice white.
Any one of numerous species of butterflies belonging to Pieris, and allied genera in which the color is usually white. See Cabbage butterfly, under Cabbage.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Whited; p. pr. & vb. n. Whiting.]

defs: To make white; to whiten; to whitewash; to bleach.
quotes: Whited sepulchers, which indeed appear beautiful outward, but are within full of . . . uncleanness.
So as no fuller on earth can white them.
